page: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, with 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark testing, the F48ams_v6 demonstrates top-tier performance in both single-core and multi-core CPU stress tests, as well as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation. Memory subsystem performance is strong, with uncached read bandwidth reaching 226.16 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 144.16 GB/sec. The system also excels in low-latency operations, delivering 112.7 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and strong performance in database operations. For AI workloads, the hardware achieves top-tier text generation speeds on medium-sized LLM models, though performance scales down to average levels for larger models.

Given its high resource density and dedicated AMD EPYC cores, the F48ams_v6 offers a highly cost-efficient profile for memory-bound enterprise workloads that do not require local SSDs. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead costs for architectures relying on remote Premium Storage. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale software compilation, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure F48ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famsv6 family, powered by a dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks position this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression, with strong memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities. It is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, Redis caching, software development pipelines, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v6 is a memory-intensive server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance optimized for databases and memory-heavy enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v6 is a memory-intensive server with 48 AMD EPYC cores and 384 GB RAM, optimized for databases and software compilation.
tagline: A memory-intensive, 48-core AMD EPYC virtual machine delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for enterprise databases.

384.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
